title: Whiplash
logline: A young drummer at a prestigious music conservatory becomes obsessed with achieving perfection under the brutal tutelage of an abusive conductor who will stop at nothing to push his students beyond their limits.
antagonistOrConflict: Fletcher (the abusive conductor) and Andrew's own obsession with perfection
themes: The thin line between passion and obsession

actBreaks.description: Andrew's world fundamentally shifts as he begins his obsessive training regimen after being accepted into Fletcher's Studio Band.

validation.ruling: Scene #31 shows Andrew at his lowest point, crying and considering his transfer application after Fletcher's brutal humiliation, representing the end of his naive phase and transition into obsessive determination.

actBreaks.description: Andrew quits drumming entirely after the car crash and confrontation with Fletcher, marking his complete withdrawal from music.

validation.ruling: Scene #84 shows Andrew's desperate arrival at the auditorium after his car crash, bloodied and injured but still determined to perform - the culmination of his obsession before his final catastrophic failure on stage.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a psychological pressure cooker, building tension through increasingly brutal rehearsal scenes that function almost like combat sequences. The structure mirrors Andrew's drumming - starting with tentative single hits and building to a frenzied, blood-soaked climax. Each act escalates the stakes: Act 1 establishes the seductive danger of Fletcher's world, Act 2 becomes a sustained torture sequence culminating in physical and emotional breakdown, and Act 3 transforms into a final confrontation where student and teacher engage in musical warfare. The pacing is relentless once Andrew enters Fletcher's orbit, with brief respites (family dinner, coffee with Nicole) serving only to highlight how consumed Andrew has become.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script accelerates dramatically after scene 21 when Andrew joins Studio Band, with rehearsal scenes growing longer and more intense. The middle section (scenes 57-71) creates sustained tension through the marathon rehearsal sequence, while the final act builds to the climactic performance through quick cuts between Andrew's preparation and the concert setup.

incitingIncident.description: Andrew falls asleep on the drum throne and wakes when Studio Band members arrive, meeting Carl who orders him to tune the drums and turn pages.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: 21 INT. GEHRING HALL - STUDIO BAND ROOM - MORNING 21 21

validation.ruling: Scene #1 is too early setup without consequences. Scene #27 shows Andrew's first brutal Studio Band rehearsal where Fletcher psychologically destroys him during 'Whiplash,' shattering his ordinary world and forcing him into the high-stakes conflict that drives the entire story.

midpoint.description: Fletcher gives Andrew a new chart called 'Caravan' with an extremely fast tempo and warns him that he must earn the core drummer position or lose it to alternates.
midpoint.sceneHeading: 52 INT. GEHRING HALL - STUDIO BAND ROOM - DAY 52 52

validation.ruling: Scene #59 perfectly captures the midpoint shift as Fletcher introduces Ryan as Andrew's replacement, making Andrew's position 'temporary' and forcing him from reactive to proactive mode - he can no longer coast but must fight to reclaim his spot.

climax.description: Andrew retreats backstage where Jim finds and hugs him, trying to take him away, but Andrew tells the stage hands he doesn't know his father and steps back toward the stage.

validation.ruling: Scene #108 shows Andrew just opening his closet to look at old drums, which is setup not climax. Scene #129 is the final extended drum solo performance where Andrew demonstrates his complete transformation and engages in the ultimate musical confrontation with Fletcher.

scenes.heading: 1 INT. NASSAU BAND REHEARSAL STUDIO - GEHRING HALL - NIGHT 1 1
scenes.summary: Andrew practices drums alone in a rehearsal room when Fletcher enters, introduces himself, and asks Andrew basic questions about his year and identity.
scenes.turningPointNote: First encounter between Andrew and Fletcher - the central relationship that will drive the entire story

scenes.heading: 2 EXT. NEW YORK STREET - SHAFFER CONSERVATORY - NIGHT 2 2
scenes.summary: Andrew exits the building and hurries off into the night, carrying a backpack covered with jazz drummer patches.

scenes.heading: 3 INT. MOVIE THEATER - LOBBY - NIGHT 3 3
scenes.summary: Andrew buys concessions at a movie theater and exchanges smiles with Nicole, the counter girl, but is too nervous to approach her romantically.

scenes.heading: 4 INT. MOVIE THEATER - MOMENTS LATER 4 4
scenes.summary: Andrew joins his father Jim at the movies and tells him Fletcher had him play, with Jim suggesting he keep other options open and sharing his own failed writing dreams.

scenes.heading: 5 OMITTED 5 5
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 6 INT. DORMITORY - HALLWAY - HOURS LATER 6 6
scenes.summary: Andrew returns to his dorm hallway where a party is happening and drug dealing is occurring, quickly retreating to his own room.

scenes.heading: 7 INT. DORMITORY - ANDREW’S ROOM - NIGHT 7 7
scenes.summary: Andrew watches a documentary about Buddy Rich in his dorm room filled with jazz memorabilia and drum equipment.

scenes.heading: 8 INT. GEHRING HALL - NASSAU BAND ROOM - DAY 8 8
scenes.summary: Andrew attends Nassau Band rehearsal and watches Ryan, the star drummer, with his attractive girlfriend before rehearsal begins.

scenes.heading: 9 INT. GEHRING HALL - NASSAU BAND ROOM - MOMENTS LATER 9 9
scenes.summary: After rehearsal, Andrew asks Mr. Kramer about his progress and Fletcher's Studio Band, but Kramer dismisses him and praises Ryan instead.

scenes.heading: 10 OMITTED 10 10
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 11 INT. GEHRING HALL - BASEMENT HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS 11 11
scenes.summary: Andrew walks down a hallway carrying a transfer application and overhears students mocking someone's embarrassing performance.

scenes.heading: INT. GEHRING HALL - STUDIO BAND ROOM - CONTINUOUS 11A
scenes.summary: Andrew watches Studio Band rehearse through glass, mesmerized by their superior playing, until Fletcher spots him and he flees.

scenes.heading: 12 INT. ANDREW’S PRACTICE ROOM - A FEW HOURS LATER 12 12
scenes.summary: Andrew practices frantically at increasingly fast tempos until his drumstick snaps, leaving him exhausted and staring at his transfer application.

scenes.heading: INT. ANDREW’S PRACTICE ROOM - MOMENTS LATER 12A
scenes.summary: Andrew listens to an impossibly fast Buddy Rich recording, considers the challenge, then turns it off and makes a decision.

scenes.heading: 13 INT. MOVIE THEATER - LOBBY - AFTERNOON 13 13
scenes.summary: Andrew nervously asks Nicole out at the movie theater, and after she jokingly rejects him, she agrees to go.

scenes.heading: 14 INT. GEHRING HALL - LOBBY OUTSIDE DEAN'S OFFICE - DAY 14 14
scenes.summary: Andrew waits outside the Dean's office with his filled-out transfer application when Fletcher's assistant calls him in to see Fletcher.

scenes.heading: 15 INT. GEHRING HALL - FLETCHER’S OFFICE - DAY 15 15
scenes.summary: Fletcher warmly discusses Andrew's transfer plans, shares a story about Charlie Parker being humiliated into greatness, then invites Andrew to join Studio Band.
scenes.turningPointNote: Fletcher recruits Andrew into Studio Band - the central arena where their psychological battle will unfold

scenes.heading: 16 INT. GEHRING HALL - NASSAU BAND ROOM - NIGHT 16 16
scenes.summary: Fletcher takes over Nassau Band rehearsal and has Andrew play drums, calling him up to perform while the room falls silent.

scenes.heading: 17 INT. PIZZERIA - NIGHT 17 17
scenes.summary: Andrew and Nicole have dinner at a pizzeria where he nervously shares jazz trivia and she teases him about his shyness, with both enjoying the conversation.

scenes.heading: 18 INT. DORMITORY - ANDREW’S ROOM - PRE-DAWN 18 18
scenes.summary: Andrew oversleeps and wakes up panicked at 5:17 AM, realizing he's late.

scenes.heading: INT. DORMITORY - HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER 18A
scenes.summary: Andrew bursts out of his room and races down the hallway.

scenes.heading: 19 EXT. DORMITORY - NEW YORK STREET - MOMENTS LATER 19 19
scenes.summary: Andrew dashes across the campus green in the pitch black, cold morning.

scenes.heading: 20 INT. GEHRING HALL - STAIRWELL - CONTINUOUS 20 20
scenes.summary: Andrew runs down the stairwell, slips and falls hard down a full flight of stairs, then gets up and keeps running despite his injuries.

scenes.heading: INT. GEHRING HALL - BASEMENT HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS 20A
scenes.summary: Andrew reaches the Studio Band room and pushes open the doors.

scenes.heading: INT. GEHRING HALL - STUDIO BAND ROOM - CONTINUOUS 20B
scenes.summary: Andrew finds the Studio Band room completely empty at 5:33 AM.

scenes.heading: INT. GEHRING HALL - BASEMENT HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS 20C
scenes.summary: Andrew discovers a sign-up sheet showing Studio Band actually starts at 9 AM, not 5:30 AM.

scenes.heading: 21 INT. GEHRING HALL - STUDIO BAND ROOM - MORNING 21 21
scenes.summary: Andrew falls asleep on the drum throne and wakes when Studio Band members arrive, meeting Carl who orders him to tune the drums and turn pages.

scenes.heading: 22 INT. GEHRING HALL - BASEMENT HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER 22 22
scenes.summary: Andrew sits in the hallway studying the complex "Whiplash" sheet music while overhearing Studio Band members mock other players, then Fletcher approaches and reassures him.

scenes.heading: 23 INT. STUDIO BAND ROOM - GEHRING HALL - MOMENTS LATER 23 23
scenes.summary: Andrew plays drums during "Whiplash" rehearsal, initially doing well and earning Fletcher's praise, but then makes a mistake and Fletcher explodes in rage, humiliating him brutally.
scenes.turningPointNote: Fletcher's first explosive attack on Andrew - reveals the true nature of their relationship and Fletcher's abusive methods

scenes.heading: 24 INT. GEHRING HALL - BASEMENT HALLWAY - DAY 24 24
scenes.summary: Andrew exits the rehearsal room trying to hold back tears, encounters Ryan who tries to congratulate him, then runs away crying.

scenes.heading: 25 INT. DORMITORY - ANDREW’S ROOM - DUSK 25 25
scenes.summary: Andrew cries alone in his dorm room, then answers a call from his father and breaks down about Fletcher ripping him apart.

scenes.heading: 26 INT. GEHRING HALL - STUDIO BAND ROOM - FLETCHER’S OFFICE - DAY 26 26
scenes.summary: Andrew enters Fletcher's office and declares his determination not to disappoint, while Fletcher watches him with interest.
scenes.turningPointNote: Andrew commits fully to pursuing Fletcher's approval rather than transferring - choosing obsession over escape

scenes.heading: 27 INT. ANDREW’S PRACTICE ROOM - DAY 27 27
scenes.summary: Andrew practices drums furiously in his practice room.

scenes.heading: 28 INT. GEHRING HALL OFFICE ROOM - DAY 28 28
scenes.summary: Andrew photocopies dense sheet music for multiple complex jazz pieces.

scenes.heading: 29 EXT. NEW YORK STREET - SHAFFER CONSERVATORY - EVENING 29 29
scenes.summary: Fletcher walks outside in the drizzle, unfolds an umbrella, and passes other faculty members.

scenes.heading: 30 INT. DORMITORY - ANDREW’S ROOM - EVENING 30 30
scenes.summary: Andrew moves his mattress and alarm clock out of his dorm room into the hallway.

scenes.heading: 31 INT. SUBWAY - NIGHT 31 31
scenes.summary: Fletcher rides the subway, looking diminutive among the other commuters.

scenes.heading: 32 EXT. NEW YORK SIDE STREET - NIGHT 32 32
scenes.summary: Andrew walks down a street eating McDonald's for dinner while listening to a metronome through earphones.

scenes.heading: 33 INT. ANDREW’S PRACTICE ROOM - NIGHT 33 33
scenes.summary: Andrew lifts weights with his right arm, then picks up drumsticks to practice his double-time swing.

scenes.heading: 34 EXT. FLETCHER’S APARTMENT BUILDING - NIGHT 34 34
scenes.summary: Fletcher arrives at his nondescript apartment building, looking like a quiet, everyday man.

scenes.heading: 35 INT. ANDREW’S PRACTICE ROOM - NIGHT 35 35
scenes.summary: Andrew sleeps in his practice room on the mattress he brought down, having essentially moved in with his belongings and alarm clock.

scenes.heading: 36 INT. FLETCHER’S APARTMENT - DINING ROOM - NIGHT 36 36
scenes.summary: Fletcher sets an elegant table for one and eats a frozen dinner alone, with a photo of his former family visible on the wall.

scenes.heading: 37 INT. ANDREW’S PRACTICE ROOM - MORNING 37 37
scenes.summary: Andrew practices obsessively with increasing metronome speeds, his hands blistering, tearing, and bleeding as he pushes to 405 BPM.

scenes.heading: 38 INT. FLETCHER’S APARTMENT - DINING ROOM - NIGHT 38 38
scenes.summary: Fletcher finishes his solitary meal and listens to melancholy music alone in his apartment filled with jazz memorabilia.

scenes.heading: 39 INT. DUNELLEN AUDITORIUM - STAGE - EVENING 39 39
scenes.summary: A rival band performs fast big band jazz on stage at the first competition of the winter season.

scenes.heading: 40 INT. DUNELLEN AUDITORIUM - GREEN ROOM - HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER 40 40
scenes.summary: Andrew watches rival players backstage and observes Fletcher warmly interacting with a technician's four-year-old daughter.

scenes.heading: 41 INT. DUNELLEN AUDITORIUM - STAGE - CONTINUOUS 41 41
scenes.summary: Studio Band takes the stage with Carl at drums and Andrew assisting, as Fletcher prepares to conduct their performance.

scenes.heading: 42 INT. DUNELLEN AUDITORIUM - GREEN ROOM - MOMENTS LATER 42 42
scenes.summary: After their performance, Carl hands Andrew the music folder to hold for the second set.

scenes.heading: 43 INT. DUNELLEN AUDITORIUM - HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER 43 43
scenes.summary: Andrew sets down the music folder to buy a Coke, overhears players discussing someone's complaint, and discovers the folder has disappeared.

scenes.heading: 44 INT. DUNELLEN AUDITORIUM - GREEN ROOM - MOMENTS LATER 44 44
scenes.summary: Carl tells Fletcher that Andrew lost the folder, and Fletcher explodes at Carl for trusting Andrew with it, leaving Carl unable to perform without the music.
scenes.turningPointNote: The lost folder crisis forces Andrew into the spotlight as the only available drummer - setting up his crucial performance moment

scenes.heading: 45 INT. DUNELLEN AUDITORIUM - STAGE - MOMENTS LATER 45 45
scenes.summary: Andrew sits at the drums on stage, overwhelmed and nervous, adjusting his kit while Carl glares at him from behind as Fletcher prepares to conduct the performance.

scenes.heading: 46 INT. DUNELLEN AUDITORIUM - STAGE - HOURS LATER 46 46
scenes.summary: Fletcher accepts an award and gives a speech about seeing Charlie Parker as a child, with Andrew now part of his winning band on stage.

scenes.heading: INT. DUNELLEN AUDITORIUM - STAGE - WINGS - CONTINUOUS 46A
scenes.summary: A stage hand returns Carl's music folder that was accidentally thrown in the trash, and Carl looks suspiciously at Andrew.

scenes.heading: 47 INT. STUDIO BAND ROOM - GEHRING HALL - DAY 47 47
scenes.summary: Fletcher dismisses Carl from the core group and tells him he can't waste time with alternates, leaving Carl confused and displaced.

scenes.heading: 48 INT. ROAD TO NEW JERSEY - BUS - DAY 48 48
scenes.summary: Andrew watches Buddy Rich videos on his phone when he receives a voicemail from Carl accusing him of deliberately losing the folder to sabotage his position.

scenes.heading: 49 INT. NEW JERSEY - JIM’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - EVENING 49 49
scenes.summary: Jim asks Andrew about Studio Band and whether Fletcher's opinion means a lot to him, with Jim's tone seeming almost accusatory.

scenes.heading: 50 INT. JIM’S HOUSE - DINING ROOM - NIGHT 50 50
scenes.summary: At a family dinner, Andrew tries to share his drumming success but is immediately overshadowed when his accomplished cousin Travis arrives, and his aunt dismisses Andrew's achievements while praising everyone else's.

scenes.heading: 51 INT. COFFEE SHOP - DAY 51 51
scenes.summary: Andrew breaks up with Nicole at a coffee shop, explaining that his musical pursuits will consume his time and make him a distracted, unreliable partner.
scenes.turningPointNote: Andrew sacrifices his personal relationship for his obsession with drumming perfection

scenes.heading: 52 INT. GEHRING HALL - STUDIO BAND ROOM - DAY 52 52
scenes.summary: Fletcher gives Andrew a new chart called 'Caravan' with an extremely fast tempo and warns him that he must earn the core drummer position or lose it to alternates.
scenes.turningPointNote: Fletcher sets up the ultimate test that will define Andrew's fate in the band

scenes.heading: 53 INT. GEHRING HALL - FLETCHER’S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS 53 53
scenes.summary: Andrew bursts into Fletcher's office demanding to talk, but Fletcher is emotional and near tears, telling Andrew he must earn the part.

scenes.heading: 54 INT. GEHRING HALL - STUDIO BAND ROOM - CONTINUOUS 54 54
scenes.summary: Andrew storms back through the studio band room with burning eyes, ignoring Ryan's invitation to lunch, focused solely on getting his part back.

scenes.heading: INT. DORMITORY - BASEMENT BATHROOM - DAY 54A
scenes.summary: Andrew fills a jug with ice water in the dormitory basement bathroom.

scenes.heading: 55 INT. ANDREW’S PRACTICE ROOM - DAY 55 55
scenes.summary: Andrew practices 'Caravan' obsessively until his hands blister and bleed, dipping them in ice water and screaming in frustration when he makes mistakes.

scenes.heading: 56 INT. SUBWAY - NIGHT 56 56
scenes.summary: Andrew studies his sheet music intensely while riding a crowded subway at night.

scenes.heading: 57 INT. GEHRING HALL - STUDIO BAND ROOM - NIGHT 57 57
scenes.summary: Fletcher plays a melancholy ballad and reveals it was written by Sean Casey, a former student who died in a car accident, creating an emotional moment in rehearsal.

scenes.heading: 58 INT. GEHRING HALL - BASEMENT HALLWAY - LATER 58 58
scenes.summary: Players rest in the hallway while Fletcher's screaming can be heard through the walls during the brutal late-night rehearsal.

scenes.heading: 59 INT. GEHRING HALL - STUDIO BAND ROOM - NIGHT 59 59
scenes.summary: Fletcher unleashes vicious verbal abuse on Andrew, calling him worthless and making cruel personal attacks as Andrew plays with bleeding hands.

scenes.heading: 60 INT. GEHRING HALL - BATHROOM - NIGHT 60 60
scenes.summary: Exhausted players rinse their faces in the bathroom during the late-night rehearsal marathon.

scenes.heading: 61 INT. GEHRING HALL - STUDIO BAND ROOM - NIGHT 61 61
scenes.summary: Fletcher forces the three drummers to rotate repeatedly through the grueling 'Caravan' piece, with Andrew finally taking his turn completely exhausted and bloodied.

scenes.heading: 62 EXT. GEHRING HALL - SIDE DOOR - NIGHT 62 62
scenes.summary: The brutal rehearsal ends at 3:30am with players staggering out like zombies, and Andrew appears fundamentally changed and completely hollowed out.
scenes.turningPointNote: Andrew is psychologically broken by Fletcher's abuse, marking his complete transformation

scenes.heading: 63 OMITTED 63 63
scenes.summary: This scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: 64 INT. ROAD TO DUNELLEN - GREYHOUND BUS - DAY 64 64
scenes.summary: Andrew practices 'Caravan' obsessively on a Greyhound bus with pills beside him when the bus suddenly jolts and crashes to a stop.

scenes.heading: 65 EXT. ROAD TO DUNELLEN - GREYHOUND BUS - DAY 65 65
scenes.summary: Andrew and other passengers wait outside the broken-down bus with a blown tire as he checks the time anxiously.

scenes.heading: 66 EXT. DUNELLEN - BUS STOP - DAY 66 66
scenes.summary: Andrew desperately searches for transportation in Dunellen, running through the streets when he learns cabs take too long and there's a car rental nearby.

scenes.heading: 67 EXT. CAR RENTAL AGENCY - MOMENTS LATER 67 67
scenes.summary: Andrew pounds frantically on the car rental agency door, which appears closed, until a man opens it for him.

scenes.heading: 68 INT. CAR RENTAL AGENCY - MOMENTS LATER 68 68
scenes.summary: Andrew hurriedly signs rental paperwork and leaves his stick bag behind on a chair as he rushes out.

scenes.heading: INT. DUNELLEN STREET - RENTAL CAR - CONTINUOUS 69A
scenes.summary: Andrew drives frantically while on the phone with the pianist who warns him Fletcher has Connolly warming up as his replacement.

scenes.heading: EXT. DUNELLEN AUDITORIUM - MOMENTS LATER 69B
scenes.summary: Andrew pulls up to the Dunellen Auditorium.

scenes.heading: 70 INT. DUNELLEN AUDITORIUM - GREEN ROOM - DAY 70 70
scenes.summary: Fletcher refuses to let Andrew play because he's late and doesn't have his sticks, giving the part to Ryan despite Andrew's desperate protests.

scenes.heading: 71 INT. CAR RENTAL AGENCY - MOMENTS LATER 71 71
scenes.summary: Andrew races back to the car rental agency and grabs his forgotten stick bag.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAR RENTAL AGENCY - MOMENTS LATER 71A
scenes.summary: Andrew runs to his rental car and speeds away from the agency.

scenes.heading: 72 INT. DUNELLEN STREET - CAR - MOMENTS LATER 72 72
scenes.summary: Andrew drives recklessly while talking on his phone and trying to turn off the GPS, becoming increasingly distracted and frantic.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. DUNELLEN STREET - CRASHED RENTAL CAR - CONTINUOUS 72A
scenes.summary: Andrew's car crashes and flips upside down, trapping his left hand under the steering wheel until he yanks it free, breaking his index finger with a sickening crack.
scenes.turningPointNote: Andrew's obsession leads to a devastating car crash and serious injury

scenes.heading: 73 I/E. DUNELLEN AUDITORIUM - LOBBY - MOMENTS LATER 73 73
scenes.summary: Andrew runs three blocks to the auditorium with his hand bleeding profusely, ignoring shocked passersby and focused only on reaching the stage.

scenes.heading: 74 INT. DUNELLEN AUDITORIUM - STAGE - MOMENTS LATER 74 74
scenes.summary: Andrew forces Ryan off the drum kit and attempts to play 'Caravan' with his broken finger, but cannot hold the stick properly and fails catastrophically on stage.
scenes.turningPointNote: Andrew's final desperate attempt to play results in complete public humiliation and failure

scenes.heading: 75 OMITTED 75 75
scenes.summary: This scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: 76 OMITTED 76 76
scenes.summary: This scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: 78 INT. ANDREW’S DORMITORY - DAY 78 78
scenes.summary: Andrew stands alone in his dorm with a bandaged hand, looking at his dismissal letter from Shaffer and watching a home video of himself as a happy eight-year-old drummer.

scenes.heading: 83 INT. ANDREW’S PRACTICE ROOM - DAY 83 83
scenes.summary: Andrew violently dismantles his drum kit in the practice room, tearing apart the drums and ripping his Buddy Rich poster to shreds.

scenes.heading: 84 INT. HALLWAY - ANDREW’S PRACTICE ROOM - MOMENTS LATER 84 84
scenes.summary: Andrew throws his drum equipment in the trash and sits in the empty hallway, then calls someone asking where to meet them.
scenes.turningPointNote: Andrew makes the decision to cooperate with the investigation against Fletcher

scenes.heading: 77 OMITTED 77 77
scenes.summary: This scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL LOBBY - BAR - LATER THAT DAY 77A
scenes.summary: Lawyer Rachel Bornholdt reveals to Andrew that Sean Casey committed suicide, not died in a car accident as Fletcher claimed, and asks Andrew to testify against Fletcher to prevent future abuse.
scenes.turningPointNote: Andrew learns the truth about Casey's death and is recruited to bring down Fletcher

scenes.heading: 79 OMITTED 79 79
scenes.summary: This scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: 80 OMITTED 80 80
scenes.summary: This scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: 81 OMITTED 81 81
scenes.summary: This scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: 82 OMITTED 82 82
scenes.summary: This scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: 85 OMITTED 85 85
scenes.summary: This scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: 86 EXT. NEW YORK STREET - BILLBOARD STREET - LATE AFTERNOON 86 86
scenes.summary: Andrew walks through New York carrying laundry and sees a billboard advertising the JVC Jazz Festival, now living far from Shaffer in the Lower East Side.

scenes.heading: 87 INT. ANDREW’S APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - LATE AFTERNOON 87 87
scenes.summary: Jim arrives at Andrew's new apartment with groceries and asks if Andrew is ready for something.

scenes.heading: 88 INT. ANDREW’S APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - LATE AFTERNOON 88 88
scenes.summary: Andrew and Jim watch 'North by Northwest' in the sparse apartment with college applications on the table, Andrew appearing sad, tired and resigned months after leaving Shaffer.

scenes.heading: 89 INT. ANDREW’S APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - EVENING 89 89
scenes.summary: Jim prepares to leave Andrew's apartment, offering to go see a movie, but Andrew declines and wants to stay home, then after Jim leaves, Andrew considers calling Nicole but pulls back scared.

scenes.heading: 90 INT. SANDWICH SHOP - DAY 90 90
scenes.summary: Andrew works at a sandwich shop, silently making and handing a ham sandwich to a customer.

scenes.heading: 91 INT. JIM’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - DAY 91 91
scenes.summary: Andrew sits apart from Uncle Frank, Jim, and Travis who are laughing and cheering at a hockey game on TV, his mind elsewhere.

scenes.heading: 92 EXT. NEW YORK STREET - JAZZ CLUB - NIGHT 92 92
scenes.summary: Andrew walks alone eating pizza, passes a street drummer without watching, then stops when he sees Fletcher's name on a jazz club sign as a guest performer.

scenes.heading: 93 INT. JAZZ CLUB - MOMENTS LATER 93 93
scenes.summary: Andrew enters the jazz club and watches Fletcher play piano with delicate, gentle keystrokes in a soft and exquisite performance that surprises Andrew.

scenes.heading: INT. JAZZ CLUB - NIGHT 93A
scenes.summary: Fletcher and Andrew sit at a table in silence, then Fletcher asks what Andrew is doing and tells him he shouldn't be sorry about Dunellen, praising Andrew for playing with a broken bone after crashing a car.

scenes.heading: 94 EXT. JAZZ CLUB - NIGHT 94 94
scenes.summary: Fletcher offers Andrew a drumming position in his JVC band as a replacement, explaining he needs someone who already knows the Studio Band playlist including Whiplash and Caravan.
scenes.turningPointNote: Fletcher offers Andrew a path back into music, setting up the final act

scenes.heading: 95 INT. ANDREW’S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - DAY 95 95
scenes.summary: Andrew opens his closet doors revealing his old drums gathering dust, his heart swelling and nerves racing at the sight.

scenes.heading: INT. ANDREW’S APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - MOMENTS LATER 95A
scenes.summary: Andrew sets up his drums with renewed energy and speed in his movements that hasn't been seen since Dunellen.

scenes.heading: INT. ANDREW’S APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - EVENING 95B
scenes.summary: Andrew practices drums for hours with sweat running down his cheeks, his eyes wide and glowing with focus as he returns to the musical life.

scenes.heading: 96 INT. JUDY AND ARTHUR ZANKEL HALL - NIGHT 96 96
scenes.summary: Andrew rehearses with Fletcher's jazz orchestra at Carnegie Hall's Zankel Hall, and after they finish Whiplash, Fletcher calmly dismisses the group and calls Andrew aside.

scenes.heading: 97 OMITTED 97 97
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 98 INT. ANDREW’S APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - HOURS LATER 98 98
scenes.summary: Andrew calls Nicole to apologize for his past behavior and invite her to his JVC performance, but she reveals she's engaged to someone else and hangs up on him.

scenes.heading: 99 OMITTED 99 99
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 100 OMITTED 100 100
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: INT. ANDREW’S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - DAY 100B
scenes.summary: Andrew gets dressed in his performance attire, putting on slacks, white shirt, black jacket and tie.

scenes.heading: 101 INT. FLETCHER’S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - DAY 101 101
scenes.summary: Fletcher dresses alone in his elegant suit with melancholy precision, playing a melody on his piano before grabbing his music folder and heading out.

scenes.heading: 102 OMITTED 102 102
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: INT. ANDREW’S APARTMENT - BATHROOM - DAY 102A
scenes.summary: Andrew prepares his hands for performance by clipping his nails, applying ointment, and wrapping each finger in a Band-Aid.

scenes.heading: 103 EXT. NEW YORK STREET - FLETCHER’S SUBWAY ENTRANCE - 103 103
scenes.summary: Fletcher ignores passing cabs and enters a subway station.

scenes.heading: 104 EXT. NEW YORK STREET - CARNEGIE HALL - DUSK 104 104
scenes.summary: Andrew emerges from a subway station, murmuring and tapping nervously as he rushes toward Carnegie Hall, then stops when he sees Fletcher approaching from the side steps.

scenes.heading: 105 INT. ZANKEL HALL - GREEN ROOM - NIGHT 105 105
scenes.summary: Andrew stands backstage among the rush of musicians and technicians preparing for the performance, checking his phone as showtime approaches.

scenes.heading: INT. ZANKEL HALL - GREEN ROOM STAIRCASE - MOMENTS LATER 105A
scenes.summary: Andrew peers through a door to catch a glimpse of the main hall.

scenes.heading: INT. ZANKEL HALL - LOBBY - CONTINUOUS 105B
scenes.summary: Andrew sees the audience in the lobby and spots his father Jim among the crowd.

scenes.heading: INT. ZANKEL HALL - GREEN ROOM STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS 105C
scenes.summary: Andrew smiles at seeing his father and walks back down the stairs feeling confident and ready to prove himself.

scenes.heading: INT. ZANKEL HALL - GREEN ROOM - MOMENTS LATER 105D
scenes.summary: Fletcher gives the band a pre-performance speech about how success tonight will open doors while failure will end careers, then dismisses them to take the stage.

scenes.heading: 106 INT. ZANKEL HALL - STAGE - CONTINUOUS 106 106
scenes.summary: The band takes the stage to applause, then Fletcher approaches Andrew and reveals he knows Andrew was the one who got him fired, having invited him here for revenge.
scenes.turningPointNote: Fletcher reveals his trap - he knows Andrew betrayed him and this is his revenge

scenes.heading: 107 INT. ZANKEL HALL - LOBBY - CONTINUOUS 107 107
scenes.summary: Jim runs down the hall toward the backstage entrance.

scenes.heading: 108 INT. ZANKEL HALL - STAGE - CONTINUOUS 108 108
scenes.summary: Andrew retreats backstage where Jim finds and hugs him, trying to take him away, but Andrew tells the stage hands he doesn't know his father and steps back toward the stage.
scenes.turningPointNote: Andrew rejects his father's rescue attempt and chooses to face Fletcher's challenge

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
